summary: Production runtime API contract v1 for production readiness.
rationale: This runtime slice makes the default Python API return a production-safe contract while preserving explicit debug APIs for CLI, TUI, and local artifact workflows. It intentionally follows the prompt-safety lane so prompt/runtime governance remains separable.
verification: uv run pytest tests/test_runtime_production_contract.py tests/test_api_production_contract.py tests/test_api.py tests/test_cli.py tests/test_tui.py -q
uv run ty check src tests tools
uv run pytest tests/test_liepin_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_opencli_worker_client.py tests/test_liepin_cli.py tests/test_liepin_boundaries.py -q
deletion_targets: public API responses that expose local run directories, trace paths, or debug artifact internals by default
implicit source selection in the production API surface
risks: This runtime lane is based on the prompt-safety lane until that PR lands; governance against origin/main should be re-run after restacking onto the updated main.
Later runtime slices must enrich source coverage, core commit receipts, degradation semantics, and constraint decisions rather than overloading this API-contract slice.
